question: Is F. prausnitzii A2-165 (= F. duncaniae) engineering-tractable for OE-relevant payloads, and which payloads rank highest by composite feasibility?

chassis.name: Faecalibacterium prausnitzii A2-165 (now F. duncaniae per Sakamoto 2022 IJSEM, doi:10.1099/ijsem.0.005379)
chassis.oxygen_tolerance: Strict obligate anaerobe (dies under aerobic conditions within minutes; though oxygen-tolerance under riboflavin/cysteine conditions has been characterized — extracellular electron transfer via riboflavin)

factor_rationales.cluster_size_tractability: Single 448-aa ORF (1.3 kb). Native gene — option to chromosomally duplicate at native locus or supplement on plasmid.
factor_rationales.GC_content_codon_compatibility: NATIVE F. prausnitzii gene. CAI = 1.0 by definition. Zero codon-optimization cost.
factor_rationales.secretion_pathway_availability: Butyryl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase is cytoplasmic. Product (butyrate) diffuses across cell membrane as a small molecule. No secretion engineering needed.
factor_rationales.host_physiology_compatibility: Native enzyme in native pathway in native host. No physiology mismatch. Anaerobic-pathway-anaerobic-host alignment is total.
factor_rationales.engineering_toolkit_maturity: Shared factor. Even for a native-payload boost, the cassette must still be integrated; transformation toolkit gap applies.
factor_rationales.host_toxicity_risk: Native overexpression risks fitness loss via diversion of acetate / acetyl-CoA from primary metabolism, especially if cassette is on a high-copy plasmid. Manageable via copy-number tuning / inducible promoter. Not host-toxic in the toxicity sense.
factor_rationales.heterologous_expression_precedent: Closely-related-chassis precedent: Clostridium tyrobutyricum, C. acetobutylicum butyrate-pathway boost via plasmid-based overexpression (biofuel literature, well-established). F. prausnitzii-specific precedent: zero. Motif maturity is high; species-level precedent is zero.
factor_rationales.folding_complexity: Native enzyme — folding in native cytoplasm is by definition the optimal case.

factor_rationales.cluster_size_tractability: 280 aa truncated construct (~840 bp). Trivially clonable.
factor_rationales.GC_content_codon_compatibility: Same human ~58% GC vs Fp 56.6%. Codon optimization moderate.
factor_rationales.secretion_pathway_availability: Sec-translocon present. Same caveat as lactoferrin re: disulfide-rich secretion. 8 disulfides in SCR1-4 — less than lactoferrin's 17 but still requires oxidative folding.
factor_rationales.host_physiology_compatibility: No O2 requirement. CR1 (a host complement regulator) is mechanistically inert to bacterial physiology — F. prausnitzii has no classical-pathway complement target.
factor_rationales.host_toxicity_risk: CR1 is a host immune regulator; no bacterial target. Native serpins / complement regulators don't perturb commensal physiology.
factor_rationales.heterologous_expression_precedent: Zero precedent for soluble complement regulator in obligate anaerobe. comp-006/012 evaluated DAF SCR1-4 in koji (HIGH risk) — informs the parallel for LBP but doesn't establish a positive precedent.
factor_rationales.folding_complexity: 8 disulfides require oxidative folding. Smaller magnitude than lactoferrin's 17 disulfides, but same fundamental anoxic-environment-folding question. SCR/sushi/CCP-fold proteins are tolerant of disulfide isomerization (more robust than serpin-fold lactoferrin) — slight upward adjustment vs. lactoferrin.

factor_rationales.cluster_size_tractability: Single 691-aa mature ORF (~2.1 kb). Larger than uricase but well within single-vector range.
factor_rationales.GC_content_codon_compatibility: Human ~58% GC vs Fp 56.6% — only 1.4 percentage point mismatch. Best GC match of any payload in this set. Codon optimization still recommended for mammalian-table -> Firmicutes-table; ~$3-5K DNA synthesis.
factor_rationales.secretion_pathway_availability: Sec-translocon annotated; native MAM secretion proves the pathway functions. But heterologous-payload secretion titers from Fp never measured. 17 disulfides in mature lactoferrin require post-translocational oxidative folding — Bacillus / Firmicutes Sec pathway produces proteins to the cell exterior where disulfide bond formation occurs (e.g. DsbA/DsbB-class systems); native F. prausnitzii disulfide machinery less characterized.
factor_rationales.host_physiology_compatibility: No O2 dependence. Iron-sequestration function aligns with anaerobic-lumen physiology (anaerobic pathogens compete for iron). Minor risk: lactoferrin's iron-sequestration could starve F. prausnitzii itself, since Fp also requires iron — manage via inducible promoter or strain-engineered iron uptake.
factor_rationales.host_toxicity_risk: Native lactoferrin doesn't kill F. prausnitzii in vivo (Fp and lactoferrin coexist in healthy gut). Heterologous overexpression risk is iron-starvation of the host, mitigatable with inducible promoter.
factor_rationales.heterologous_expression_precedent: Extensive precedent in non-anaerobe heterologous hosts (P. pastoris, A. niger, transgenic mammals). Zero precedent in obligate anaerobe. comp-005 evaluated koji chassis HIGH risk for stability — LBP track was framed precisely to address this.
factor_rationales.folding_complexity: MAJOR concern. 17 disulfides require oxidative folding. Gut lumen is anoxic — disulfide bond formation in anoxic environment requires alternative oxidant (e.g. periplasmic-equivalent space + heterologously expressed DsbA, or methodologically untested in obligate anaerobes). Disulfide-rich secreted protein expression in obligate anaerobes is essentially unprecedented.

factor_rationales.cluster_size_tractability: Single 302-aa ORF (906 bp), trivial cluster size.
factor_rationales.GC_content_codon_compatibility: A. flavus CDS ~52% GC vs Fp 56.6%. Small GC mismatch, eukaryotic codon-table mismatch. Codon optimization moderate-cost (~$1-2K DNA synthesis).
factor_rationales.secretion_pathway_availability: Uricase is cytoplasmic/peroxisomal natively; functions intracellularly. No secretion engineering needed. But this raises the OPPOSITE problem: substrate (uric acid) must enter cell, product (allantoin) must exit.
factor_rationales.host_physiology_compatibility: DOMINANT BLOCKER. Uricase requires O2 as substrate. F. prausnitzii is a strict anaerobe in an anoxic colonic lumen. Heterologous H2O2 production from uricase activity ALSO toxic to the anaerobic host (H2O2 lethal to obligate anaerobes without catalase, which F. prausnitzii lacks).
factor_rationales.engineering_toolkit_maturity: Shared factor across payloads. No published F. prausnitzii transformation; closest precedent is Lachnospiraceae conjugation (Roseburia, E. rectale per Sheridan 2019/2020). Estimated 1-2 yr adaptation needed.
factor_rationales.host_toxicity_risk: Uricase produces stoichiometric H2O2 per uric acid molecule. F. prausnitzii lacks robust catalase; H2O2 destroys obligate anaerobes via [Fe-S] cluster damage. Adding heterologous catalase as a co-payload is engineerable but compounds the campaign.
factor_rationales.heterologous_expression_precedent: Zero precedent for uricase in obligate anaerobe. Rasburicase (S. cerevisiae expressing A. flavus uricase) is the canonical engineering precedent — aerobic / fermentation-batch context, fundamentally different physiology.
factor_rationales.folding_complexity: Cofactorless single-domain enzyme. No disulfides, no metals, no glycosylation. Folding in bacterial cytoplasm is well-precedented (Rasburicase in S. cerevisiae).

scoring_method: Eight feasibility factors per payload (0.0 catastrophic to 1.0 no obstacle). Geometric mean across factors. Geometric mean punishes any single very-low factor more than arithmetic mean — appropriate epistemology when every factor must succeed.
shared_blocker: The engineering-toolkit-maturity factor applies to ALL payloads at value ~0.25 — F. prausnitzii has no published transformation protocol as of 2026-05. This is a FIXED FACILITY cost that gets paid once for any engineering campaign, not a per-payload cost. Conditional on the toolkit being established (an estimated 1-2 yr investment), the per-payload feasibility re-ranks differently — see summary.md 'Toolkit-conditional ranking' section.
